content:
About Nebulo # Nebulo is a platform where we strive to list the best websites in their respective fields – websites that are SAFE and share our PRINCIPLES and VALUES, namely a FREE and OPEN internet for everyone.
Our Mission # We firmly believe that:
Governments should not monitor our messages Android should not be a locked-in platform forever Video games should still be playable 80 years after purchase DRM should not ruin the gaming experience Companies should not force us to log in every 30 days to renew a licence We are fighting for people’s privacy, for people’s freedom.
Our Code of Ethics # We have a code of ethics that is very rare these days, but which was the norm in the early days of piracy – not to mention the C-word.
This simple code of ethics is effective; it states, for example, that a video game player should, where possible, buy the game to support the developers.
Because our fight is not against the developers, but against the big companies.
Today, big companies have monopolies everywhere, which wasn’t the case before—or at least it wasn’t obvious.
The Problem with Big Companies # Take the cases of Ubisoft, Blizzard and Nintendo, where their executives have been involved in multiple incidents of sexual assault against women, yet these cases always end without any consequences.
Companies that are absolutely determined to release a game by a deadline. I’ll take the only exception that springs to mind: Cyberpunk 2077.
The single best game of the last decade, which was ruined by investors who were desperate for the game to be released on 22 December before Christmas to boost sales.
The result: it was the worst video game launch of the decade. Yet, a few years later, it is undeniably the best in its category (that’s my opinion; everyone is entitled to their own).
Nintendo: A Case Study # Take Nintendo, for example, which is releasing a Switch 2 that looks exactly the same as the Switch 1, with components that were already outdated at launch, and some absolutely disgraceful practices:
You can no longer lend out your cartridges without risking having your console banned Pokémon games are just as rubbish as ever, since they’re more interested in making money than putting a smile on their customers’ faces Just look at Pokémon Legends Arceus, where it was a major change and we needed it, and the game is as dead as my two grandparents put together, the landscape is empty, etc.
In short (we could also take the example of Pokémon Legends Z-A).
The Indie Revolution # I haven’t come here to air my views on the latest games released; I just want to explain that we simply want the companies to listen to us, and they’ve got everything to gain from it.
When you see that Silksong has smashed sales records on Steam – in fact, Steam actually crashed because there were so many sales – and let’s not forget it’s an indie game…
That says it all.
Our Ethos # IN SHORT, our ethos is simple:
Try before you buy, donate if you can, enjoy it if you like.
We’re not the enemies of the big players, just a bunch of random people who want to create a space for the smaller ones.
We are looking for new partners. Your website simply needs to be different from those listed. You can use the contact form below to get in touch with us.
Contact # For questions, suggestions, or collaborations:
Email: nebulo.greedless451@passmail.net Last updated: April 2026
title: About Us

content:
Welcome to Nebulo, a group dedicated primarily to preserving video games, but in the future we plan to expand to include all sorts of things, such as apps and software, movie download sites, abandonware games, and more. We’re looking for partners—feel free to contact us! In the meantime, here are our partner sites:
Axekin Browse, discover and track games for your favorite consoles and emulators. Your ultimate retro gaming companion.
Browse Games AnkerGames Free Download Pre-installed portable PC games from steam and major publishers with fastest downloads.
Browse Games
